question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: responseTimeout field] Can an unprivileged external user cause a denial-of-block-range-queries by submitting transactions that keep block nodes consistently busy beyond responseTimeout, so that every getBlockRange() call times out, all nodes return EMPTY_BLOCK_RANGE, BlockNodeSubscriber.getNode() throws BlockStreamException, and all records are missing from the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: maxBlockItems field / BlockAssembler.append()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the maxBlockItems check in append() compares pendingCount (total items across all BlockItemSets) against maxBlockItems, so that a block node that splits a large block into many small BlockItemSets accumulates items until the limit is hit, throwing BlockStreamException and causing the block's records to be missing?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: maxBlockItems field] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the interaction between maxBlockItems and the block assembly logic in BlockAssembler, where a block with exactly maxBlockItems+1 items always triggers BlockStreamException, causing that specific block's records to be permanently missing from the mirror node with no retry mechanism for the dropped block?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: maxStreamResponseSize field] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that maxStreamResponseSize is applied as the gRPC channel's maxInboundMessageSize at channel creation time in BlockNode constructor, meaning that if a block node sends a single SubscribeStreamResponse larger than this limit, the gRPC framework throws an exception before the application can process it, causing the block to be dropped and its records to be missing?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: maxStreamResponseSize field] Can an unprivileged external user submit a smart contract transaction with a very large return value or event log that causes the corresponding BlockItemSet to exceed maxStreamResponseSize, causing the gRPC channel to reject the message, the block to be dropped, and the contract execution records to be missing from the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: maxSubscribeAttempts field / BlockNode.onError()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that onError() increments errors atomically but the check errors.incrementAndGet() >= maxSubscribeAttempts is not synchronized with the active flag update, creating a race condition where multiple concurrent errors could each see the threshold exceeded and each set a new readmitTime, potentially extending the deactivation window beyond readmitDelay?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: maxSubscribeAttempts field] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that maxSubscribeAttempts errors are counted per BlockNode instance, and when BlockNodeSubscriber.getBlockNodes() recreates nodes due to a configuration change, the error counter resets to zero, allowing an attacker to repeatedly trigger configuration-change-induced node recreation to reset error counters and avoid detection while still causing intermittent missing records?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: readmitDelay field / BlockNode.tryReadmit()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the tryReadmit(false) call in BlockNodeSubscriber.getNode() which only readmits a node if Instant.now().isAfter(readmitTime), so that if the attacker causes deactivation with a long readmitDelay, the node remains inactive for the full duration and all blocks in that window are missing from the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: readmitDelay field] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the interaction between readmitDelay and the force-readmit path in BlockNodeSubscriber.getNode() (tryReadmit(true)), where a node is force-readmitted only if it has the required block, so that if the attacker causes all active nodes to not have the required block, the force-readmit path is never triggered and records are permanently missing?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: maxBlockItems field] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the BlockStreamException thrown by append() when maxBlockItems is exceeded propagates through streamBlocks() to onError() in BlockNode, incrementing the error counter, so that a sustained attack of oversized blocks eventually deactivates the node and causes a readmitDelay-length window of missing records?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: maxBlockItems field] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the default maxBlockItems=800_000 by observing that on a network with high transaction throughput, legitimate blocks may naturally approach this limit, and any additional transactions submitted by the attacker push the count over the threshold, causing legitimate records to be missing from the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: responseTimeout field] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that responseTimeout is used both for the serverStatus deadline in getBlockRange() and as the block assembly timeout in BlockAssembler.timeout(), so that a single misconfigured or too-small responseTimeout value simultaneously breaks block range discovery and block streaming, causing all records to be missing from the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: responseTimeout field] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the BlockAssembler.timeout() logic where stopwatch.start() is called on the first BlockItemSet, so that if the first BlockItemSet arrives at time T and the block has many more items, the remaining timeout is responseTimeout minus elapsed time, and a slow block node causes the timeout to expire mid-block, dropping the block and causing missing records?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: maxStreamResponseSize field] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that maxStreamResponseSize is set as maxInboundMessageSize on the gRPC ManagedChannel, which applies to all messages on that channel including both serverStatus responses and block stream responses, so that a large serverStatus response exceeding maxStreamResponseSize causes getBlockRange() to fail and the node to return EMPTY_BLOCK_RANGE, causing missing records?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: maxSubscribeAttempts field] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the errors counter in BlockNode is shared across all block numbers, so that causing maxSubscribeAttempts errors on different blocks (not the same block) still deactivates the node, allowing an attacker to use a low-rate attack spread across multiple blocks to avoid detection while still causing node deactivation and missing records?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: maxBlockItems field / maxSubscribeAttempts field] Can an unprivileged external user chain the maxBlockItems and maxSubscribeAttempts limits by submitting exactly enough transactions to exceed maxBlockItems on exactly maxSubscribeAttempts consecutive blocks, causing the node to be deactivated after exactly 3 blocks, creating a predictable and repeatable pattern of missing records on the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: readmitDelay field / maxSubscribeAttempts field] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the combination of maxSubscribeAttempts=3 and readmitDelay=1 minute to create a 1-minute window of missing records every time they cause 3 consecutive errors, and by timing these attacks to coincide with high-value transactions, ensure that specific records are missing from the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: maxBlockItems field] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the BlockStreamException message 'Too many block items in a pending block: received %d, limit %d' reveals the current pendingCount and maxBlockItems configuration, allowing the attacker to calibrate their transaction submission rate to reliably exceed the limit without triggering other defenses?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: maxStreamResponseSize field] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that maxStreamResponseSize defaults to 36MB but the @NotNull constraint only prevents null values (not zero or very small values), so that if the configuration is set to DataSize.ofBytes(1), every block response is rejected by the gRPC channel and all records are missing from the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: responseTimeout field] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that responseTimeout has only a @DurationMin(millis = 100) lower bound with no upper bound, so that if misconfigured to a very large value (e.g., hours), a slow or unresponsive block node holds the gRPC read call open for hours, blocking the single-threaded block processing loop and causing all subsequent blocks' records to be missing from the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: maxBlockItems field] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that maxBlockItems has no upper bound constraint (@Min(1000) only), so that if misconfigured to Integer.MAX_VALUE, the limit is effectively disabled and a block node can send an unlimited number of block items, potentially causing an OutOfMemoryError in BlockAssembler's pending list and causing all records to be missing?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: maxSubscribeAttempts field] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that maxSubscribeAttempts has no upper bound constraint (@Min(1) only), so that if misconfigured to Integer.MAX_VALUE, a node is never deactivated regardless of errors, causing the mirror node to keep retrying a broken node indefinitely and missing all records that could have been fetched from a healthy fallback node?
